design_problem: Heterogeneous personal monitoring data cannot be reused reliably when withheld source media, reports, daily summaries and session observations are flattened.
design_objective: Represent modality, temporal level, source device and processing status explicitly.
artifact_response: Schema, data dictionary, long/wide tables, raw/intermediate/processed layer separation.
claim_boundary: Supports schema-governed curation in the demonstration package; does not claim universal health-data modelling.

design_problem: Repository availability alone does not make a package reusable.
design_objective: Package code, metadata, manifests and documentation as auditable information-system objects.
artifact_response: README, reproducibility guide, manifest, datapackage metadata, validation reports.
claim_boundary: Supports repository-level inspectability; does not certify production readiness.

design_problem: Self-tracking data remain privacy-sensitive after direct identifiers are removed.
design_objective: Make release decisions visible and separate public, conditional, restricted and withheld materials.
claim_boundary: Supports privacy-risk-aware release governance; does not guarantee anonymisation.

design_problem: Executable scripts can be mistaken for exact reproducibility.
design_objective: Report reproducibility by scope rather than as an undifferentiated PASS.
artifact_response: Smoke test, validator, run_all execution and output-equivalence audit.
claim_boundary: Supports executable reproduction and selected exact equivalence; full output equivalence is not claimed.

design_problem: A single self-tracking dataset can be misread as clinical evidence.
design_objective: Keep the demonstration case analytically bounded and focus evaluation on artifact functionality.
artifact_response: Non-clinical interpretation rules, limitations and supplementary case description.
claim_boundary: Supports information-systems artifact evaluation; does not support diagnosis or population inference.
